mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical difference from zero during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and corporate earnings deterioration, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of months where UNRATE crosses above 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal income growth outpacing consumer price inflation expands real purchasing power and corporate profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS growth and CPIAUCSL growth yields a negative out-of-sample coefficient, or the joint signal predicts negative returns in over 50 percent of quarters.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rising policy rates, an accelerating industrial production index confirms resilient economic output that offsets higher discount rates, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns when FEDFUNDS increases by more than 0.25 percent in the prior quarter.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing housing starts signal expanding construction activity and durable goods demand, which directly boosts corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: HOUST growth shows zero correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple market cycles, or Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 200, persistent inflation forces central banks to tighten policy, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of months where CPIAUCSL remains above 200.0.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market tightening that supports wage growth and consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE produces negative forward returns in more than 50 percent of observed quarters, or the interaction term loses statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, decelerating real GDP growth signals peak economic activity and impending recession, causing investors to discount future earnings and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: GDPC1 deceleration fails to predict negative 20-day returns when the yield curve is steeply positive, or the signal produces positive returns in over 55 percent of triggered instances.
